items.title: Search ongoing for man believed to have jumped off Banting bridge
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT: Search and rescue operations are ongoing for a 29-year-old man believed to have jumped off a bridge in Banting, with no sign of the victim despite an extensive multi-agency effort. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the operation had entered its second day today, with firemen assisted by police, the Special Tactical Operations and Rescue Team (Pantas) and the department's K9 unit continuing to comb the area around the Banting bridge along Jalan Muhibbah 9, Taman Muhibbah. He said monitoring patrols by boat covered about 5km from the search base, while the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) from the KLIA Fire and Rescue Station, together with personnel from the Banting station, conducted surface searches towards Teluk Datok.

items.title: Iran FM warns any challenge to Hormuz routes will 'increase tensions'
items.summary: BAGHDAD: Iran's top diplomat warned Sunday that any attempt to bypass the Strait of Hormuz routes agreed with the United States would "increase tensions" in the Middle East, as the countries traded attacks and accusations of violating a fragile ceasefire in the region. During a visit to Iraqi capital Baghdad, Abbas Araghchi also called for the establishment of a security framework with Gulf countries, with Teheran and Washington accusing each other of violating the fragile truce that was meant to end the Middle East war. Araghchi's warning came after the US military said it carried out new strikes Saturday on multiple targets in Iran, in response to a fresh attack on a ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Johor polls: Age no barrier to service, say youngest and oldest candidates
items.summary: Pakatan Harapan's Danish Hossman Abd Rahman, 23, is the youngest candidate in the polls and will contest the Johor Lama state seat. Danish, campaigning as Danish Rahman, said that despite his youth, he had gained valuable knowledge from working with experienced leaders, which had matured him. "I want to offer an energetic youth workforce and new ideas for the voters here in Johor Lama," he said today.

items.title: Pakatan’s youngest candidate Danish Hossman takes cue from PM Anwar in Johor Lama showdown
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 28 — The resilience and staunch principles of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s struggle served as the main ‘tonic’ to inspire Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Danish Hossman Abd Rahman to enter politics at the young age of 23. Contesting the Johor Lama State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seat in the 16th Johor State Election (PRN), Danish Hossman, who is the youngest candidate, described the PH Chairman as his political ‘compass’ — one who has remained steadfast in championing the welfare of the people despite facing various storms of trials. “To be honest, I only have one idol in this political arena, Datuk Seri Anwar.

items.title: Amir Hamzah unveils RM5.5m sea ambulance, promises faster patient transfers from Langkawi
items.summary: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the initiative aimed to strengthen access to faster and more efficient emergency healthcare services, particularly for the Langkawi community and tourists. Amir Hamzah, who is also co-chairman of the Langkawi Development Authority (LADA), said the sea ambulance would be operated by Hospital Sultanah Maliha (HSM) here, with its primary role being the transfer of patients from the hospital to the mainland. He said the sea ambulance service would also ensure the health, safety and privacy of patients during transfers.

items.title: What we know about the foreign victims of Venezuela's quakes
items.summary: CARACAS: The latest death toll from twin earthquakes that rattled Venezuela on Wednesday stands at 1,430, and foreign nationals have been confirmed among the dead. Seven Chinese Seven Chinese nationals were among the victims, Beijing's state broadcaster CCTV reported Saturday, citing figures from the embassy in Caracas. Nine Spaniards At least nine Spanish nationals have died, 131 are missing and 14 others have been found under the rubble, the foreign ministry said on Sunday.

items.title: Suspects in high-speed chase that caused crash in KL remanded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The two men arrested after leading police on a high-speed chase before crashing into three vehicles along Jalan Kuching have been remanded for five days to facilitate investigations. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the remand order runs from June 27 to July 1. He said investigations revealed that police seized 19.63g of suspected heroin from the Perodua Viva involved in the incident, with initial findings indicating the drugs were intended for the suspects' personal use.

items.title: Vance, Rubio strike different tone on Iran and Israel
items.summary: PRESIDENT Donald Trump's administration has pushed hard to present a united front on the Iran war, but statements by his vice president and secretary of state have at times diverged over the past week, especially on the subject of Israel. Vice President JD Vance, speaking at the White House last week, lashed out against Israeli critics of the preliminary US-Iran deal. Pressed on Vance's criticism, Rubio deflected before recounting an assault by the Lebanon-based militia on an Israeli checkpoint earlier in the week.

items.title: Zahid says Johor must remain BN stronghold, brushes off Puad’s recycled‑candidates criticism
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 28 — Johor must remain a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold and continue to serve as the coalition’s political fixed deposit in the state election, said BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said a BN victory in Johor would mark the beginning of Umno’s and BN’s tangible resurgence, while reaffirming the strength of the party, which is now 80 years old. Ahmad Zahid said the momentum to restore BN’s influence must be driven comprehensively across the state to demonstrate the enduring strength of grassroots support.

items.title: Driver charged over SPE flyover crash that killed motorcyclist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 21-year-old has been charged over a crash on the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) that killed a motorcyclist who plunged 30m from a flyover. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the driver, a woman, was charged at the magistrate's court on Friday. "The court fixed Sept 10 for document submission," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Thunderstorm, heavy rain forecast for KL, Putrajaya and 10 states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecast to affect Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm today. In a statement today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected areas include Perlis, Penang, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda and Kulim), and Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar and Bagan Datuk). The same warning has also been issued for Sarawak, covering Bau and Kuching in Kuching Division; Serian; Samarahan; Sri Aman; Betong; Sarikei; Selangau in Sibu Division; Mukah; Song and Kapit in Kapit Division; Tatau in Bintulu Division; and Subis and Miri in Miri Division.

items.title: Pahang Sultan, Tengku Ampuan extend condolences to family of Tok Luar Datuk Jamaluddin
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah and Tengku Ampuan of Pahang Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah have conveyed their condolences to the family of Tok Luar Datuk Jamaluddin Ahmad, who died this morning. According to a post on the Kesultanan Pahang Facebook page, Their Royal Highnesses expressed deep sadness over Jamaluddin's passing, describing him as a loyal supporter of the Pahang Sultanate institution. Jamaluddin died at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) at 6.48am today.

items.title: France records 1,000 excess deaths during record-breaking heatwave
items.summary: PARIS: France has recorded 1,000 excess deaths during the blistering heatwave sweeping Europe, the public health agency said today, warning that the true figure was likely to be higher. Detailing its preliminary count of excess deaths, Sante Publique said most of the fatalities involved older people and that it expected the mortality rate to rise as more information became available about deaths in residential care and homes. EXTREME HEAT EASES IN FRANCE The heatwave has been moving east.

items.title: Publishers must review books with Quranic and hadis content carefully, says Jakim
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 28 — The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) has urged the National Book Council of Malaysia to continuously remind publishers to exercise utmost caution in the publication of religious books containing Quranic verses and hadis texts. Its director-general Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee said in a statement today that JAKIM, together with the state Islamic religious authorities, is ready to provide the necessary guidance to publishers of religious books to ensure compliance with Islamic requirements. Sirajuddin said the department supports the firm stance of Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Dr Mohd Na’im Mokhtar that publishers must uphold the sanctity of Islam and ensure religious books are vetted by the relevant authorities before publication.

items.title: Johor polls: Two media centres and 100 NADI set up to aid coverage, MCMC to curb provocative content, says Teo
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — The Communications Ministry, in collaboration with the Information Department (JaPen), has set up two main media centres and 100 National Information Dissemination Centres (NADI) across Johor to support media coverage of the 16th Johor state election. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the two main centres, located at Hotel Seri Malaysia in Johor Bahru and NADI Kampung Sawah Awok in Muar, operate daily from 9 am to 9 pm from June 26 until polling day on July 11. On telecommunications coverage during the campaign period, Teo said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) would monitor telecommunications companies to ensure internet speeds remain at optimum levels.

items.title: Johor polls can trigger Umno-Pas unity through strategic voting, says Akmal
items.summary: MELAKA: Umno Youth has welcomed Pas's suggestion for Perikatan Nasional (PN) supporters to vote for Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in seats where the coalition is not contesting in the upcoming Johor election. He was commenting on a statement by Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, who urged PN supporters to vote for BN candidates in Johor election seats not contested by the coalition to block Pakatan Harapan (PH) from winning. Following this, he said Umno hopes the Pas machinery can campaign for BN candidates, particularly in areas contested by Bersatu.

items.title: BIS says debt, AI boom and fragilities raise global risks
items.summary: LONDON: Global pressures ranging from rising public debt to financial fragilities and the sustainability of the artificial intelligence (AI) boom are increasing risks, underscoring the need for disciplined policymaking, according to the Bank for International Settlements (BIS). While economic activity has remained resilient in recent months, policymakers must act decisively to preserve stability, the BIS said. Ultimately, success depends on sound fiscal and financial foundations," BIS General Manager Pablo Hernandez de Cos said.

items.title: Loke: Underused LRT3 car parks to make way for affordable housing under TOD plans
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Vacant Prasarana-owned car parks along the Shah Alam LRT3 line will be redeveloped into high-density affordable housing and commercial lots under transit-oriented development (TOD) plans aimed at encouraging train commuting and optimising land use around rail stations. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative aims to facilitate high-density living near stations, ensuring that more commuters settle within walking distance of their daily rail route. He said several areas along the LRT3 corridor, including Sri Andalas, Kayu Ara, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia, have been identified for potential TOD projects involving housing and commercial centres.

items.title: Johor election: Disabled man's creativity draws attention
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Losing his left leg due to diabetes in 2019 has not dampened Abd Karim Md Taha's spirit, who continues to brighten his home with creative decorations themed around current events. The 59-year-old former lorry driver said his interest in home decoration began nearly 10 years ago, and ahead of National Day celebrations or other major events, he would transform the colour scheme and garage of his house according to selected themes. He said among the themes he has created are Merdeka celebrations, elections, Malaysia Madani, and football seasons, including painting his house blue and red in support of Johor Darul Ta'zim (JDT).

items.title: Pakatan fielding youngest candidate in Johor polls as part of youth‑focused push, says Fahmi
items.summary: MUAR, June 28 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is committed to advancing its youth agenda in the 16th Johor state election by translating young people’s aspirations into meaningful representation through a slate of credible young candidates. PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition had demonstrated this commitment by fielding a number of young professionals, including the youngest candidate in the election. Also present was PH candidate for the Sungai Balang seat, Ayna Soraya Badaruddin.

items.title: Johor polls: It's not just about MB candidate, says PKR youth leader
items.summary: PKR Youth deputy chief Nabil Halimi said speculation over the next menteri besar was misplaced. Earlier, Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said Johor deserved more than just a menteri besar candidate, but a credible governing team led by a competent and visionary leader, in an apparent swipe at Pakatan Harapan for declining to name its chief ministerial candidate. "Perhaps Azleen has forgotten that Johor's 'poster boy' in the last state election also did not become menteri besar," Nabil said.

items.title: Police arrest six 17-year-olds over bullying of 14-year-old student in Muar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — Six male students, aged 17, have been arrested to assist in investigations into a bullying incident involving a 14-year-old student at a school in Muar last May. Johor Police Chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the boys were arrested in Muar and Alor Gajah, Melaka, between 1.30 am and 2.12 am today, following a report lodged yesterday. He said the police took the bullying incident at the school seriously and would conduct a transparent and fair investigation.

items.title: Hassan's appointment as Rembau Undang followed adat, no palace interference
items.summary: REMBAU: The appointment of the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, Hassan Ab Hamid, was conducted independently without any royal interference from Istana Seri Menanti in Negri Sembilan. Datuk Juan Datuk Zulkipli Shamsudin, chairman of the Kerapatan Buapak Lapan Kampung Waris Biduanda Dua Carak said that under Adat Perpatih (matrilineal custom), the selection of the Undang of Rembau remains the exclusive right of Luak Rembau, following established processes without royal interference in the selection of the ruling chief. Zulkipli said the post fell vacant following the passing of the 21st Undang of Rembau, Datuk Lela Maharaja Datuk Muhamad Sharip Othman, who died of old age at 82 on May 15, 2024.

items.title: As Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak rages, knowledge gaps still challenge response
items.summary: AS scientists race to understand the growing Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo, medics say knowledge gaps are still hampering detection and care for sick patients. Since the outbreak was declared in mid-May, Congo's government has reported more than 1,100 cases and almost 300 deaths, by far the biggest outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola to date. Ebola Bundibugyo is distinct from the better-known Ebola Zaire, which caused the largest-ever outbreak in West Africa in 2014 to 2016.

items.title: At 103, 'Death Railway' survivor still walks strong in Felda
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: At the age of 103, Kastawi Esa continues to walk on his own — carrying with him memories of surviving forced labour on the brutal 'Death Railway' under Japanese occupation. He said life during the nearly three years there was extremely harsh, with forced labour carried out in dense jungle conditions and very limited food supplies, surviving mainly on river water. "We worked under Japanese military control.

items.title: Johor campaign sees shift from street banners to digital outreach
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: On the second day of the 14-day Johor election campaign period, physical campaign materials were noticeably subdued, with fewer flags, banners and posters along major routes compared with previous elections. The New Straits Times observed that traditional campaign visuals, once a defining feature of election seasons, are now less visible on the ground. Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the reduced physical presence reflected a change in campaign methods rather than a decline in intensity.

items.title: Fahmi: Multi‑operator tower to improve internet coverage in Sungai Balang Darat by Q3
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the ministry had identified the need for the digital infrastructure project and had been working with CelcomDigi on its implementation since the end of last year. He said the tower would be equipped with Multi Operator Core Network (MOCN) technology, allowing all major telecommunications providers to share the network and ensuring comprehensive coverage. Insya-Allah, internet connectivity issues in several critical locations in Sungai Balang will be addressed immediately,” he said when met at the Jiwa@Komuniti Madani Sembang Santai World Cup Edition programme at Pasar Awam Parit Jawa here today.

items.title: Baghdad Green Zone hit by massive raids as MPs, officials held
items.summary: BAGHDAD: Iraqi security forces launched raids in Baghdad's fortified Green Zone early Sunday to arrest officials including MPs suspected of corruption, amid heavy deployment in the area. High-ranking officials and politicians also live there. AFP correspondents reported heavy security deployment at the entrances of the Green Zone.

items.title: Thai family mourns teen girl found dead in suitcase as Australian arrested
items.summary: BANGKOK: The family of a 17-year-old Thai girl whose body was found in a suitcase in Pattaya said they were devastated by her death, for which an Australian man has been arrested and charged with murder. Thai police said they arrested an Australian man in his 40s at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi airport early on Saturday in connection with the killing in Pattaya, about 150 km (93 miles) east of Bangkok. Thai police said they reviewed CCTV footage that showed Carman entering a condominium with the girl, then leaving alone hours later carrying a suitcase.

items.title: NADI teams to reach out and empower communities through digital economy, says Fahmi
items.summary: Its Minister, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said the move is to ensure more people receive additional information and training through programmes provided by NADI. “I will ask NADI teams to reach out and assist families to receive additional information and training, for example to become TikTok affiliates or sell products online. “I also ask that NADI be proactive in going out, not merely waiting for people to come to NADI.

items.title: Johor polls: Bersama ready to take on BN, PH in Johor Jaya
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Bersama Johor Jaya candidate Lau Yi Leong is unfazed by the risk of losing his deposit in the upcoming 16th Johor polls. "However, based on the positive feedback from Johor Jaya voters, I am confident we can challenge the other candidates, especially from PH and BN. The Johor Jaya seat features a four-cornered fight involving PH, BN, Bersama and an independent candidate.

items.title: Japan mulls expanding govt bonds for individuals
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's Finance Ministry is considering expanding its lineup of government bonds available to individual investors, with experts pushing for new products such as inflation-linked bonds amid rising prices, its officials say. During a meeting among experts on managing government bonds, some attendees called for offering super long-term bonds with 20- or 30-year maturities to individual investors. The two others are five-year fixed-rate bonds and three-year fixed-rate bonds, whose interest rates remain unchanged until maturity.

items.title: Zahid urges end to polemic over Puad's statement
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has urged all quarters to end the polemic surrounding a statement made by Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi. He said the former Umno Supreme Council member's statement on the Barisan Nasional candidates contesting in the Johor polls is just his personal opinion. Instead, the former Johor state assembly speaker said his decision was made in protest against the candidate selection system practised by BN.

items.title: Iran, US continue escalating attacks, recriminations over peace deal
items.summary: Shortly after President Donald Trump warned the US might "militarily complete the job", Iran early on Sunday launched missiles and drones on US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain, continuing a series of escalating attacks. The US military said earlier it had struck Iran again, hours after a tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most important energy shipping route, which Iran had largely cut off for most of the conflict. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said its navy and air forces had launched missile and drone operations targeting US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain in response to recent US strikes against Iran.

items.title: Eight injured in three‑vehicle crash on North-South Expressway near Sungai Petani
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Eight people were injured in a three‑vehicle collision at Kilometre 94 of the southbound North–South Expressway this morning. According to Bernama, incident involved a lorry, a Perodua Myvi and a Toyota Noah, according to Guar Chempedak Fire and Rescue Station chief Mohd Happyzam Abd Rasid, who confirmed the details in a statement. He said firefighters received the emergency call at 8.56am and found two men in the lorry, two women in the Myvi, and a man and three women in the Toyota Noah.

items.title: Johor election: Zahid tells Pas to turn words into votes for BN
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pas should translate its words into action if the Islamist party is sincere about throwing its support behind Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates contesting in the Johor election, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. The BN chairman, who is also UMNO president, said if Pas was sincere, it should translate those words into votes for BN candidates contesting in the election. "It is good if they support all 56 BN candidates contesting in Johor.

items.title: 'Make me look good': Ahmad Maslan asks reporter for a touch-up before Johor election presser
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A spontaneous moment of humour between a veteran politician and members of the media brought a touch of levity to the hectic Johor election campaign trail. Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan lightened the mood ahead of a press conference when he jokingly asked journalists if his face was sweaty and needed a touch-up before cameras started rolling. journalist Mohd Sharkawi Londing said Ahmad had asked for someone to help to wipe away his sweat, after which the interaction turned into a light-hearted exchange.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast for KL, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecast to affect Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm today. In a statement today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected areas include Perlis, Penang, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda and Kulim), and Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar and Bagan Datuk). The same warning has also been issued for Sarawak, covering Bau and Kuching in Kuching Division; Serian; Samarahan; Sri Aman; Betong; Sarikei; Selangau in Sibu Division; Mukah; Song and Kapit in Kapit Division; Tatau in Bintulu Division; and Subis and Miri in Miri Division.

items.title: Johor election victory crucial for Umno revival, says Zahid
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Umno must secure victory in the Johor polls as the starting point for the party's revival after a difficult period following the 14th General Election (GE14) eight years ago. Its president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, said victory for Umno and BN was also crucial to ensure Johor remains the party's 'fixed-deposit' (stronghold). Also present were BN secretary-general Datuk Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, BN candidate for Parit Yaani Datuk Najib Samuri, and BN candidate for Parit Raja Rashidah Ramli.

items.title: Budi Madani portal receives 13,000 new applications as off 11am - MOF
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The official Budi Madani portal has received over 13,000 new applications as of 11am today for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month for eligible private pick-up truck and jeep owners. The Finance Ministry (MOF), in a statement today, said this addition increases the total diesel subsidy quota to 300 litres per month for eligible vehicle owners. "The first day of the early access rollout for the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) initiative for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia on June 27 ran smoothly.

items.title: Pakatan files police report after party flag burned in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: PH’s candidate for the Tanjung Surat state seat, Faizul Abdul Ghani, said the incident was discovered by the head of its logistics unit at about 2am today. He said that his representative, Mohamad Saat Sukami, then lodged a report with the police and the Election Commission (EC). Yusof said police take seriously any acts of mischief, provocation or sabotage that could affect public order throughout the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Delivery dispute leads to assault on rider in Petaling Jaya, police say
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police believe a misunderstanding over a food delivery, coupled with alcohol consumption, led to the assault of a food delivery rider in Kelana Jaya early on Friday. Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Superintendent M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said investigations found the incident was believed to have stemmed from a dispute between the victim and the suspects over the delivery. "We are also tracking down another suspect believed to have been involved in the assault," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing state, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on ‘recycled’ candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration. According to Mohamed Khaled, fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Proposal for 17 new Sarawak seats unlikely this year as EC procedures ongoing
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — The proposal to add 17 new seats to the Sarawak Legislative Assembly is unlikely to be tabled in the Dewan Rakyat this year, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said the matter must go through several processes and procedures under the Election Commission (EC), including public display exercises to allow members of the public to submit objections or feedback. “At the earliest, the EC can first display it to the public.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says open debates not Johor’s way after offer from Maszlee Malik
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 28 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reportedly said open debates between candidates may not be suitable for the Johor state election, adding that they could disrupt the harmony of the campaign period. According to Buletin TV3 , he said Johor’s political culture has long placed importance on decorum, restraint and orderly debate, rather than open exchanges that could turn confrontational. This comes after Pakatan Harapan’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Datuk Maszlee Malik, had expressed his willingness to debate any party, particularly Onn Hafiz, during the Johor campaign period.

items.title: Malaysia shortlists four countries for new LCS anti-ship missile system, says defence minister
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has shortlisted four countries as possible suppliers of an anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project after Norway cancelled the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the selection process will focus on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational needs of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) beyond basic specifications. Mohamed Khaled said the government is reviewing the delivery timeline to avoid delays that could affect the country’s maritime defence capabilities.

items.title: Govt mulls affordable housing on LRT3 park-and-ride sites to boost public transport use
items.summary: KLANG, June 28 — The government is planning to redevelop large park-and-ride sites along the newly launched LRT3 Shah Alam Line into affordable housing and commercial projects, in a move aimed at making better use of public land while encouraging greater use of public transport. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposal would focus on selected stations with sizable parking facilities, rather than every station along the line, with Sri Andalas station serving as the initial example. He said Prasarana had identified large parcels of land around some stations that are currently used solely as surface car parks, despite their development potential.

items.title: Police arrest four men, seize roosters and gambling items in Mukah cockfighting raid
items.summary: MUKAH, June 28 — Four local men were arrested for allegedly involved in cockfighting activities during a police raid at an open area within an oil palm plantation along Jalan Bayan Ulu here yesterday. Mukah district police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said the raid was carried out at about 2pm by officers from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Mukah District Police Headquarters (IPD Mukah), following intelligence and public information. “Police also seized three live roosters, one dead chicken, two cockfighting spurs, a weighing scale, various gambling paraphernalia, and RM55 in cash,” he said.

items.title: Father’s role important in shielding children from social ills as Madani govt bolsters family‑focused policies, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said efforts to empower parenting, particularly fathers’ involvement in children’s upbringing and education, must continue to be prioritised as part of national efforts to build stable families, a harmonious society and a safer future for the next generation. The text of his speech was read out by Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying at the 2026 National Father’s Day celebration organised by the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) here today. Fadillah said the Madani Government has always placed the family institution as the foundation of national strength, and in line with this aspiration, the National Family Compassion Policy (Dasar KASIH Keluarga Negara), launched earlier, serves as an important guide in strengthening family institutions in the country.

items.title: Kota Samarahan to serve as alternative venue for major state events as Kuching nears saturation, says state minister
items.summary: KOTA SAMARAHAN, June 28 — Kota Samarahan is being positioned as an alternative hub for major state events through the upcoming Samarahan Boulevard development, as Kuching approaches saturation, said Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. Abdul Karim, who is also Asajaya assemblyman said Kuching is increasingly congested when hosting large-scale events, particularly at Padang Merdeka. “Looking at Samarahan, which still has ample available land, I have proposed that the area be planned as a long-term venue for major state-level events,” he said when officiating the Samarahan Food and Cultural Festival here last night.

items.title: Customs Dept seizes over RM800,000 in untaxed cigarettes in four raids in Terengganu and Kelantan
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 28 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) seized white cigarettes and keretek (clove cigarettes) worth RM807,718.12, including taxes, in four separate raids conducted between April 16 and May 3. He said inspection of the premises uncovered 10,000 cigarettes worth an estimated RM9,020, including taxes. Inspection of the vehicle found 200,000 white cigarettes,” he said.

items.title: Strong primary care key to sustainable healthcare system as hospitals face rising demand, says deputy health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Strong primary care is essential to achieving better health outcomes and building a more sustainable healthcare system in Malaysia. Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib said hospitals alone could no longer shoulder the country’s growing healthcare demands, making collaboration among healthcare providers, academia and professional bodies increasingly important. “Malaysia’s healthcare system must become more sustainable and more responsive to changing population needs.

items.title: BN considers itself underdog in Johor polls, intensifies outreach ahead of July 11, says Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is approaching the 16th Johor state election as the underdog and will not take the contest for granted despite a smooth nomination process across all 56 seats and a strong show of support from party members. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also BN’s Machap candidate, said the coalition would continue to work hard and intensify its outreach efforts to engage as many voters as possible ahead of polling day on July 11. “That is the Johor way.

items.title: PM Anwar: Free LRT3 Shah Alam Line rides for one month starting tomorrow
items.summary: KLANG, June 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced that commuters can ride the newly launched LRT3 Shah Alam Line for free for a month beginning tomorrow. Anwar said the decision followed discussions between the Transport Ministry and the Finance Ministry, acknowledging that while the free rides would incur additional costs, the long-term benefits outweighed the expense. The Finance Ministry naturally looks at the additional costs involved,” he said.

items.title: ‘Thank you PAS’: Dr Akmal welcomes directive to back Umno-BN in Johor seats where Perikatan absent
items.summary: SHAH ALAM — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has welcomed PAS’ call for its members and supporters to vote for Umno-BN candidates in state seats not contested by PN in the 16th Johor state election. “Thank you PAS. PAS had reportedly issued the directive, with its deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man quoted as saying that he was confident party members understood the need for Muslim unity.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for six states across Peninsular, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for six states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 3pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia are Kuala Muda in Kedah; the Southwest, Northeast and Seberang Perai Utara districts in Penang; Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau in Negeri Sembilan; and the whole of Melaka. In Sabah, the affected areas are Telupid, Beluran and Sandakan in the Sandakan division, as well as Pitas and Kudat in the Kudat division.

items.title: STAR Sabah Wiranita Bingkor leaders quit, citing party direction and women’s role
items.summary: KENINGAU, June 28 — Parti Solidariti Tanah Airku (STAR Sabah) Bingkor Division’s Wiranita chief Jennifer Epil, who also serves as the party’s central Wiranita vice executive committee member, has announced her resignation and withdrawal from the party with immediate effect. In a statement today, Jennifer said her decision was supported by several members of the STAR Sabah Wiranita Bingkor Division committee, who unanimously agreed to leave the party after careful and mature consideration. At the same time, Jennifer expressed her appreciation and gratitude to the STAR Sabah leadership for the trust, opportunities, and experience extended to her throughout her involvement in the party.

items.title: DOSM: 2026 Economic Census enumeration in Sabah passes 60pc mark amid strong stakeholder support
items.summary: To further improve the response rate, DOSM Sabah recently held an EC2026 engagement session with professional bodies and business associations at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC) here. In 2024, Sabah’s GDP stood at RM84.3 billion, with the services sector remaining the largest contributor at RM44.2 billion, followed by mining and quarrying (RM18.6 billion), agriculture (RM12.2 billion), manufacturing (RM6.1 billion) and construction (RM3 billion). “Based on the findings of the 2023 Economic Census, Sabah recorded 68,267 business establishments, generating a gross output of RM182.5 billion and value added of RM96.9 billion.

items.title: Flood evacuees fall to 16 in Selangor; Perak centre sees small rise to 52
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — The situation continues to improve in the flood-hit area of Selangor, while Perak recorded a slight increase in the number of evacuees this morning. In SELANGOR,16 people from 10 families are being accommodated at a relief centre at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) RTB Bukit Changgang as at 8 am, compared to 309 people from 150 families last night. In PERAK, the number of evacuees still taking shelter at the Dewan Bandaran Gopeng in the Kampar district since June 20 rose slightly to 52 people from 16 families, compared with 51 people from 16 families last night.

items.title: Batik Air to launch twice‑daily Bintulu–KL flights from July 20, says tourism minister
items.summary: BINTULU, June 28 — The flight frequency between Bintulu and Kuala Lumpur will increase to twice daily beginning July 20, said Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing. According to the Bintulu MP, with the new schedule, passengers will have access to two daily flights from Kuala Lumpur to Bintulu, departing at 10am and 2.30pm. Flights from Bintulu to Kuala Lumpur will depart at 1.10pm and 5.40pm.
